runtime_expectation: DB-backed race tests run in integration-all because they require dockerized PostgreSQL; representative unit tests remain in the unit lane when they prove pure idempotency-key or scheduler policy behavior.

determinism_policy.primary_synchronization: Use database uniqueness, transactions, claim tokens, asyncio.Event barriers, and explicit callback outcomes. Do not rely on unbounded sleeps as the primary race trigger.
determinism_policy.state_assertions: Race tests must assert final durable state, emitted/published event count when applicable, epoch or version behavior when applicable, and operator-visible status or retry state when the workflow owns one.
determinism_policy.lineage: Concurrency evidence must keep correlation, idempotency key, claim token, outbox id, job id, portfolio id, security id, and epoch visible enough for support diagnosis without exposing source payloads.

scenarios.description: Concurrent claims of the same processed-event fence converge to one durable processed_events row.
protected_failure_modes: double outbox staging from duplicate business command
operator confusion from duplicate processed-event rows

scenarios.description: Representative persistence consumers derive idempotency from transaction business identity even when Kafka transport offsets differ.
protected_failure_modes: duplicate processing on Kafka redelivery with a new offset
transport-offset-only idempotency for transaction persistence consumers
scenarios.remaining_scope: Issue sgajbi/lotus-core#553 remains the global migration for every event consumer family that still uses transport-offset fences.

scenarios.description: Valuation and scheduler tests preserve epoch and stale-skip behavior when price-driven recalculation work overlaps for the same security/date window.
Cost and position tests preserve transaction lifecycle, lot offsets, epoch propagation, and duplicate-event skips for same portfolio/security lot windows.
state_assertions: cost/outbox rows are created once for accepted events
lot and accrued-income offset state remain consistent

scenarios.description: Worker claim and stale-reset paths fence job ownership and preserve operator-visible retry/failure state.
state_assertions: only one worker claims a pending job

scenarios.description: Outbox dispatch accounts per-message delivery results and preserves retry metadata when one callback succeeds and another fails.
state_assertions: failed rows remain PENDING or FAILED according to retry policy
claim_token and claim_expires_at clear only for owned updates
protected_failure_modes: batch success hiding partial publish failure
claim-lost dispatcher marking reclaimed rows processed

scenarios.description: Pipeline and replay tests prove duplicate business-event claims skip registration while replay audit remains support-visible.
state_assertions: duplicate claims do not register stage state

scenarios.description: Transaction lifecycle and cost tests preserve correction/cancellation-style zero-quantity and no-outbox behavior during recalculation windows.
state_assertions: no unsupported correction outbox is emitted
protected_failure_modes: unsupported correction published as successful recalculation
stale cost output after cancellation-like update
scenarios.remaining_scope: Broader corporate-action correction/cancellation golden scenarios remain tracked by sgajbi/lotus-core#607.
